provenance.title: Dissecting Characteristics Nonparametrically

authors.name: Michael Weber
authors.affiliation: University of Chicago Booth School of Business, NBER

provenance.abstract: We propose a nonparametric method to test which characteristics provide independent information for the cross section of expected returns. We use the adaptive group LASSO to select characteristics and estimate how they affect expected returns nonparametrically. Our method handles large numbers of characteristics, allows flexible functional forms, and is insensitive to outliers.
key_claims: Adaptive group LASSO selects characteristics and estimates nonparametric conditional mean functions for expected returns
Only 7–15 of 36 characteristics provide independent information for expected returns depending on specification
Nonparametric model achieves 50% higher out-of-sample Sharpe ratios than the linear model (3.42 vs 2.26)
Portfolio sorts are a special case of nonparametric regression using constant splines
Significant time variation exists in the predictive power of characteristics for expected returns

architecture.description: Statistical/econometric methodology, not a neural architecture. The 'model' is an additive nonparametric regression estimated via adaptive group LASSO with quadratic splines.

components.name: Rank Normalizer
components.role: Transforms raw characteristics to cross-sectional ranks in [0,1]
components.input: Raw characteristic matrix C [N_t x S]
components.output: Rank-normalized characteristic matrix C_tilde [N_t x S]

components.name: Quadratic Spline Basis Expander
components.role: Expands each normalized characteristic into L+2 basis functions (quadratic splines with L-1 interior knots)
components.output: Basis function vector p(c_tilde) of length L+2

components.name: Additive Model Aggregator
components.role: Combines S component functions into total expected return estimate
components.input: S spline expansions, each of length L+2

components.name: Group LASSO Stage 1
components.role: Initial variable selection — penalizes entire spline groups per characteristic
components.input: Stacked design matrix X [NT x S(L+2)], return vector R [NT x 1]
components.output: First-stage coefficient vector beta_tilde [(L+2) x S]
components.operation: argmin over b: sum_i(R_it - sum_s sum_k b_sk p_k(C_tilde))^2 + lambda1 * sum_s ||b_s||_2

components.name: Adaptive Weight Calculator
components.role: Computes adaptive weights from Stage 1 to downweight already-zero groups
components.operation: w_s = (sum_k beta_tilde_sk^2)^{-1/2} if nonzero, else infinity

components.name: Adaptive Group LASSO Stage 2
components.role: Model-selection-consistent second step; correctly zeroes irrelevant characteristics
components.input: Design matrix X, returns R, adaptive weights w_s
components.operation: argmin over b: sum_i(R_it - ...)^2 + lambda2 * sum_s w_s ||b_s||_2

components.name: OLS Re-estimator
components.role: Re-estimates coefficients by OLS on selected characteristics to achieve oracle efficiency
components.input: Selected characteristic indices, design matrix, returns
components.output: Final coefficient estimates beta_hat, estimated functions m_hat_ts

components.name: Confidence Band Constructor
components.role: Produces uniform confidence bands for estimated conditional mean functions
components.output: Pointwise bands [m_hat(c) ± d_ts * sigma_hat(c)] for c in [0,1]
components.operation: Simulation-based critical values d_ts from sup|Z'p(c)/sqrt(p(c)'Sigma_hat p(c))|

components.name: Hedge Portfolio Constructor
components.role: Forms long-short portfolios based on predicted returns for evaluation
components.output: Equally-weighted hedge portfolio (long top decile, short bottom decile)

architecture.primary_variant: 14 knots for in-sample; 9 knots for out-of-sample

equations.name: Conditional Mean Function
equations.role: Defines the object of estimation — expected excess return conditional on characteristics

equations.name: Additive Model
equations.role: Core model specification — additively separable in rank-transformed characteristics

equations.name: Rank Transformation
equations.role: Maps raw characteristics to cross-sectional ranks in (0,1)

equations.name: Spline Basis Functions
equations.role: Quadratic spline basis with L-1 interior knots at quantiles t_l = l/L

equations.name: Group LASSO Stage 1 Objective
equations.role: First-step group LASSO — selects groups (characteristics) jointly

equations.name: Adaptive Weights
equations.role: Ensures Stage 2 does not select any characteristic not selected in Stage 1

equations.name: Adaptive Group LASSO Stage 2 Objective
equations.role: Model-selection-consistent step; correctly identifies truly zero functions

equations.name: Estimated Conditional Mean Function
equations.role: Final function estimate after OLS re-estimation on selected characteristics

equations.name: Uniform Confidence Band
equations.role: Uniform (sup-norm) confidence bands based on simulated critical values

tensor_semantics.note: This is a panel econometrics method; 'tensors' are matrices/vectors. No GPU tensor operations.

data_objects.name: Returns matrix
data_objects.description: Monthly excess returns for N firms over T months

data_objects.name: Raw characteristics matrix
data_objects.description: S=36 characteristics per firm per month

data_objects.name: Rank-normalized characteristics
data_objects.description: Cross-sectional rank transforms of C, values in (0,1)

data_objects.name: Spline design matrix
data_objects.description: Spline basis expansion of C_tilde; L+2 basis functions per characteristic

data_objects.name: Final coefficients
data_objects.description: OLS re-estimated coefficients for selected S_selected characteristics

data_objects.name: Predicted returns
data_objects.description: In-sample and out-of-sample predicted expected returns

training_pipeline.note: Estimation procedure, not neural network training. No optimizer in the ML sense.

estimation_steps.name: Model selection via adaptive group LASSO
estimation_steps.details: Two-step LASSO: Stage 1 with BIC-selected lambda1, Stage 2 with adaptive weights and BIC-selected lambda2

estimation_steps.name: OLS re-estimation on selected characteristics
estimation_steps.details: Pooled time-series cross-sectional OLS using only selected characteristics; run on rolling 120-month windows

estimation_steps.name: Rolling window prediction
estimation_steps.details: 10-year (120-month) estimation window, rolled forward 1 month at a time for OOS predictions starting Jan 1991

datasets.name: CRSP Monthly Stock File
datasets.description: Common stocks of US firms on NYSE/Amex/Nasdaq, price > $5

datasets.name: Compustat
datasets.description: Balance-sheet accounting data merged to CRSP; 6-month lag requirement (Fama-French timing convention)

metrics.name: In-sample Sharpe Ratio
metrics.description: Annualized Sharpe ratio of equally-weighted 10-1 hedge portfolio based on predicted returns

metrics.name: Out-of-sample Sharpe Ratio (EW)
metrics.description: OOS equally-weighted hedge portfolio Sharpe ratio

implementation_assumptions.assumption: BIC criterion follows Yuan & Lin (2006) group LASSO BIC formulation
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper cites Yuan and Lin (2006) for BIC selection

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Pooled OLS (not Fama-MacBeth) for re-estimation step
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper states 'pooled time-series cross-sectional regression'; Fama-MacBeth would be equivalent when characteristics are time-invariant but pooled is described
alternatives: Fama-MacBeth (numerically equivalent when characteristics are constant)

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Heteroscedasticity-consistent (HC) standard errors (White) for confidence bands
Confidence band critical values d_ts are computed via 10,000 simulation draws from N(0, Sigma_hat)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard practice for sup-norm bands; paper describes simulation approach without specifying draw count

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Interior knots are placed at equally-spaced quantiles t_l = l/L
Python implementation using numpy/scipy/sklearn; paper uses MATLAB (inferred from figure style)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Figure style and NBER working paper era suggests MATLAB; Python is more reproducible

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Equally-weighted portfolios use simple average returns within decile
Fama-French 3 factors sourced from Ken French data library
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard in empirical finance; paper does not specify source

ambiguities.description: The group LASSO penalty exponent: paper writes (sum_k b_sk^2)^{1/2} which is the L2 group norm. Some implementations use squared L2 norm. The L2 group norm (not squared) is standard GLASSO.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: L2 group norm (sqrt of sum of squares)

ambiguities.description: Exact CRSP/Compustat merge timing for balance-sheet variables described as 'fiscal year ending t-1' with 6-month lag but exact monthly lag cutoffs not fully specified for all 36 variables
ambiguities.primary_assumption: 6-month lag for all accounting variables following Fama-French 1993 convention

ambiguities.description: Number of simulation draws for critical value computation not stated
